briefdescription.para: Read the given Buffer Descriptor word.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the BD to read
is the word offset to be read

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XAxiDma_BdRead(u32 BaseAddress, u32 Offset)

briefdescription.para: Write the given Buffer Descriptor word.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the BD to write
is the word offset to be written
is the 32-bit value to write to the field

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XAxiDma_BdWrite(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: Write the given Buffer Descriptor word.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the BD to write
is the word offset to be written
is the 64-bit value to write to the field

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XAxiDma_BdWrite(u64 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u64 Data)

briefdescription.para: Zero out BD specific fields.

detaileddescription.para: BD fields that are for the BD ring or for the system hardware build information are not touched.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

briefdescription.para: Get the control bits for the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

simplesect.para: The bit mask for the control of the BD

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the status of a BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

simplesect.para: Word at offset XAXIDMA_BD_DMASR_OFFSET. Use XAXIDMA_BD_STS_*** values defined in xaxidma_hw.h to interpret the returned value

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the length field value from the given BD.

detaileddescription.para: The returned value is the same as what was written with XAxiDma_BdSetLength(). Note that in the this value does not reflect the real length of received data. See the comments of XAxiDma_BdSetLength() for more details. To obtain the actual transfer length, use XAxiDma_BdGetActualLength().

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on.
is the Maximum Transfer Length.

simplesect.para: The length value set in the BD.

briefdescription.para: Set the ID field of the given BD.

detaileddescription.para: The ID is an arbitrary piece of data the application can associate with a specific BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on
is a 32 bit quantity to set in the BD

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XAxiDma_BdSetId(XAxiDma_Bd* BdPtr, void Id)

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the ID field of the given BD previously set with XAxiDma_BdSetId.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

briefdescription.para: Get the BD's buffer address.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

briefdescription.para: Check whether a BD has completed in hardware.

detaileddescription.para: This BD has been submitted to hardware. The application can use this function to poll for the completion of the BD.
This function may not work if the BD is in cached memory.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to check on

listitem.para: XAXIDMA_BD_STS_COMPLETE_MASK if completed, may contain XAXIDMA_BD_STS_*_ERR_MASK bits.

briefdescription.para: Get the actual transfer length of a BD.

detaileddescription.para: The BD has completed in hw.
This function may not work if the BD is in cached memory.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to check on
is the Maximum Transfer Length.

memberdef.name: XAxiDma_BdSetTId
memberdef.initializer: { \ u32 val; \ val = (XAxiDma_BdRead((BdPtr), XAXIDMA_BD_MCCTL_OFFSET) & \ ~XAXIDMA_BD_TID_FIELD_MASK); \ val |= ((u32)(TId) << XAXIDMA_BD_TID_FIELD_SHIFT); \ XAxiDma_BdWrite((BdPtr), XAXIDMA_BD_MCCTL_OFFSET, val); \ }

briefdescription.para: Set the TID field of the TX BD.

detaileddescription.para: Provides a stream identifier and can be used to differentiate between multiple streams of data that are being transferred across the same interface.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on
is a 8 bit quantity to set in the BD

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XAxiDma_BdSetTId(XAxiDma_Bd* BdPtr, void TId)

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the TID field of the RX BD previously set with XAxiDma_BdSetTId.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

memberdef.name: XAxiDma_BdSetTDest
memberdef.initializer: { \ u32 val; \ val = (XAxiDma_BdRead((BdPtr), XAXIDMA_BD_MCCTL_OFFSET) & \ ~XAXIDMA_BD_TDEST_FIELD_MASK); \ val |= ((u32)(TDest) << XAXIDMA_BD_TDEST_FIELD_SHIFT); \ XAxiDma_BdWrite((BdPtr), XAXIDMA_BD_MCCTL_OFFSET, val); \ }

briefdescription.para: Set the TDEST field of the TX BD.

detaileddescription.para: Provides coarse routing information for the data stream.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on
is a 8 bit quantity to set in the BD

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XAxiDma_BdSetTDest(XAxiDma_Bd* BdPtr, void TDest)

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the TDest field of the RX BD previously set with i XAxiDma_BdSetTDest.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

memberdef.name: XAxiDma_BdSetTUser
memberdef.initializer: { \ u32 val; \ val = (XAxiDma_BdRead((BdPtr), XAXIDMA_BD_MCCTL_OFFSET) & \ ~XAXIDMA_BD_TUSER_FIELD_MASK); \ val |= ((u32)(TUser) << XAXIDMA_BD_TUSER_FIELD_SHIFT); \ XAxiDma_BdWrite((BdPtr), XAXIDMA_BD_MCCTL_OFFSET, val); \ }

briefdescription.para: Set the TUSER field of the TX BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on
is a 8 bit quantity to set in the BD

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XAxiDma_BdSetTUser(XAxiDma_Bd* BdPtr, void TUser)

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the TUSER field of the RX BD previously set with XAxiDma_BdSetTUser.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

memberdef.name: XAxiDma_BdSetARCache
memberdef.initializer: { \ u32 val; \ val = (XAxiDma_BdRead((BdPtr), XAXIDMA_BD_MCCTL_OFFSET) & \ ~XAXIDMA_BD_ARCACHE_FIELD_MASK); \ val |= ((u32)(ARCache) << XAXIDMA_BD_ARCACHE_FIELD_SHIFT); \ XAxiDma_BdWrite((BdPtr), XAXIDMA_BD_MCCTL_OFFSET, val); \ }

briefdescription.para: Set the ARCACHE field of the given BD.

detaileddescription.para: This signal provides additional information about the cacheable characteristics of the transfer.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on
is a 8 bit quantity to set in the BD

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XAxiDma_BdSetARCache(XAxiDma_Bd* BdPtr, void ARCache)

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the ARCACHE field of the given BD previously set with XAxiDma_BdSetARCache.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

memberdef.name: XAxiDma_BdSetARUser
memberdef.initializer: { \ u32 val; \ val = (XAxiDma_BdRead((BdPtr), XAXIDMA_BD_MCCTL_OFFSET) & \ ~XAXIDMA_BD_ARUSER_FIELD_MASK); \ val |= ((u32)(ARUser) << XAXIDMA_BD_ARUSER_FIELD_SHIFT); \ XAxiDma_BdWrite((BdPtr), XAXIDMA_BD_MCCTL_OFFSET, val); \ }

briefdescription.para: Set the ARUSER field of the given BD.

detaileddescription.para: Sideband signals used for user defined information.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on
is a 8 bit quantity to set in the BD

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XAxiDma_BdSetARUser(XAxiDma_Bd* BdPtr, void ARUser)

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the ARUSER field of the given BD previously set with XAxiDma_BdSetARUser.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

memberdef.name: XAxiDma_BdSetStride
memberdef.initializer: { \ u32 val; \ val = (XAxiDma_BdRead((BdPtr), XAXIDMA_BD_STRIDE_VSIZE_OFFSET) & \ ~XAXIDMA_BD_STRIDE_FIELD_MASK); \ val |= ((u32)(Stride) << XAXIDMA_BD_STRIDE_FIELD_SHIFT); \ XAxiDma_BdWrite((BdPtr), XAXIDMA_BD_STRIDE_VSIZE_OFFSET, val); \ }

briefdescription.para: Set the STRIDE field of the given BD.

detaileddescription.para: It is the address distance between the first address of successive horizontal reads.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on
is a 32 bit quantity to set in the BD

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XAxiDma_BdSetStride(XAxiDma_Bd* BdPtr, void Stride)

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the STRIDE field of the given BD previously set with XAxiDma_BdSetStride.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

memberdef.name: XAxiDma_BdSetVSize
memberdef.initializer: { \ u32 val; \ val = (XAxiDma_BdRead((BdPtr), XAXIDMA_BD_STRIDE_VSIZE_OFFSET) & \ ~XAXIDMA_BD_VSIZE_FIELD_MASK); \ val |= ((u32)(VSize) << XAXIDMA_BD_VSIZE_FIELD_SHIFT); \ XAxiDma_BdWrite((BdPtr), XAXIDMA_BD_STRIDE_VSIZE_OFFSET, val); \ }

briefdescription.para: Set the VSIZE field of the given BD.

detaileddescription.para: Number of horizontal lines for strided access.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on
is a 32 bit quantity to set in the BD

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XAxiDma_BdSetVSize(XAxiDma_Bd* BdPtr, void VSize)

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the STRIDE field of the given BD previously set with XAxiDma_BdSetVSize.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on

briefdescription.para: The XAxiDma_Bd is the type for a buffer descriptor (BD).
Set the length field for the given BD.

detaileddescription.para: Length has to be non-zero and less than LengthMask.
For TX channels, the value passed in should be the number of bytes to transmit from the TX buffer associated with the given BD.

For RX channels, the value passed in should be the size of the RX buffer associated with the given BD in bytes. This is to notify the RX channel the capability of the RX buffer to avoid buffer overflow.
The actual receive length can be equal or smaller than the specified length. The actual transfer length will be updated by the hardware in the XAXIDMA_BD_STS_OFFSET word in the BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on.
is the requested transfer length
is the maximum transfer length

simplesect.para: This function can be used only when DMA is in SG mode

briefdescription.para: Set the BD's buffer address.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on
is the address to set

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if buffer address set successfully
XST_INVALID_PARAM if hardware has no DRE and address is not aligned

simplesect.para: This function can be used only when DMA is in SG mode

briefdescription.para: Set the BD's buffer address when configured for Micro Mode.

detaileddescription.para: The buffer address should be 4K aligned.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on
is the address to set

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if buffer address set successfully
XST_INVALID_PARAM if hardware has no DRE and address is not aligned

simplesect.para: This function can be used only when DMA is in SG mode

briefdescription.para: Set the APP word at the specified APP word offset for a BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on.
is the offset inside the APP word, it is valid from 0 to 4
is the value to set

listitem.para: XST_INVALID_PARAM under following error conditions: 1) StsCntrlStrm is not built in hardware 2) Offset is not in valid range

simplesect.para: If the hardware build has C_SG_USE_STSAPP_LENGTH set to 1, then the last APP word, XAXIDMA_LAST_APPWORD, must have non-zero value when AND with 0x7FFFFF. Not doing so will cause the hardware to stall. This function can be used only when DMA is in SG mode

briefdescription.para: Get the APP word at the specified APP word offset for a BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on.
is the offset inside the APP word, it is valid from 0 to 4
is to tell the caller whether parameters are valid

simplesect.para: The APP word. Passed in parameter Valid holds 0 for failure, and 1 for success.
This function can be used only when DMA is in SG mode

briefdescription.para: Set the control bits for a BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on.
is the bit value to set

simplesect.para: This function can be used only when DMA is in SG mode

briefdescription.para: Dump the fields of a BD.

parameterdescription.para: is the BD to operate on.

simplesect.para: This function can be used only when DMA is in SG mode
